source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure Amazon ECS clusters are tagged

input_row.source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure Amazon ECS clusters are tagged
input_row.description: Ensure all Amazon ECS clusters have resource tags to facilitate asset management, tracking, and compliance.
input_row.rationale: Consistent tagging supports compliance and helps identify unauthorized or misconfigured resources. Impact: There is minimal administrative overhead associated with implementing and maintaining resource tags.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Login to the ECS console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/ecs/. 2. In the left panel, click Clusters. 3. Click the name of a cluster. 4. Click Tags. 5. Ensure at least one tag is listed that does not begin with aws:. Tags prefixed with aws: are AWS-managed. 6. Repeat steps 1-5 for each ECS cluster. From Command Line: Run the following command to list clusters: aws ecs list-clusters Run the following command to view the tags for a cluster: aws ecs list-tags-for-resource --resource-arn <service-arn> Ensure that tags are returned that do not begin with aws:. Tags prefixed with aws: are AWS-managed. Repeat for each cluster.
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Login to the ECS console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/ecs/. 2. In the left panel, click Clusters. 3. Click the name of a cluster. 4. Click Tags. 5. Click Manage tags. 6. Click Add tag. 7. Provide a Key and optional Value for the tag. 8. Click Save. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each ECS cluster requiring remediation. Default Value: By default, Amazon ECS clusters have only AWS-managed tags. References: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/ecs-using- tags.html 2. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/ecs/list- clusters.html 3. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/ecs/list- tags-for-resource.html
input_row.impact: There is minimal administrative overhead associated with implementing and maintaining resource tags.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit instructions provide specific AWS CLI commands (`aws ecs list-clusters` and `aws ecs list-tags-for-resource`) to check if all Amazon ECS clusters have resource tags. This indicates that the necessary information is accessible via AWS APIs.
- The checks required to determine the presence of tags are standard AWS service properties that can be accessed programmatically.
- Both boto3 SDK and AWS CLI have the necessary functionalities to list ECS clusters and their respective tags.
- This control does not require any manual intervention that involves visual inspection or human judgment beyond verifying tags which is API accessible.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_ecs_cluster_resource_tagging_compliance

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use `boto3` to list all ECS clusters with `boto3.client('ecs').list_clusters()`.
- For each cluster, retrieve the tags using `boto3.client('ecs').list_tags_for_resource(ResourceArn=cluster_arn)`.
- Validate that each cluster has at least one tag that does not start with the prefix `aws:`, which indicates a non-AWS-managed tag.
- Pass criteria: Every ECS cluster must have at least one custom tag. Clusters with only AWS-managed tags (tags that start with `aws:`) should be flagged as non-compliant.
- Summarize findings for all ECS clusters within the account and regions where checks were conducted.
